message:
🌱 Add harbor: Docker 20.10 proxy cache pull failure mission (#757)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "harbor-13740-docker-20-10-pull-proxy-cache-image-failed-error-response-from-daem",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "harbor: docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header",
+    "description": "docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header. This issue affects 23+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review harbor configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor\nkubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor\n```\nshould pull proxy cache repo images success."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Harbor to include the manifest descriptor fix",
+        "description": "Upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the ManifestExist fix:\n```bash\n# Update the Harbor Helm repo\nhelm repo update harbor\n\n# Upgrade Harbor\nhelm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml\n\n# Verify pods restart successfully\nkubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor\n```\nAfter upgrading, Docker 20.10+ clients should be able to pull proxy cache images without errors."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error\u2026 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The issue was caused by Harbor's ManifestExist method returning only a digest string instead of a full Descriptor. Docker 20.10 requires the full manifest descriptor (including mediaType and size) for proxy cache pulls. The fix updates ManifestExist to return a Descriptor instead of just a digest, allowing Docker 20.10 clients to correctly pull images through Harbor's proxy cache.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# docker.io is a proxy cache project\ndocker pull ${HARBOR_HOST}/docker.io/library/alpine:3.12\nError response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header",
+        "docker --version\nDocker version 18.06.3-ce, build d7080c1\n\ndocker pull my-harbor-domain/dockerhub/library/alpine:3.12\nError response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header",
+        "0 hochguertelto@de-dus-nb-toh:~\n$ docker --version\nDocker version 20.10.0, build 7287ab3"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "harbor",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "harbor"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/13740",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/13751"
+    },
+    "reactions": 23,
+    "comments": 21,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:20:02.594Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
